title: Slow Cooker Chicken Tortilla Soup (High-Protein Recipe) | Eati
introduction: Slow Cooker Chicken Tortilla Soup is a comforting high-protein option with chicken, beans, tomatoes, and warm spices. Let the slow cooker do the work, then log servings in Eati for accurate macros.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- Chicken
- Onion
- Garlic
- Chicken broth
- Diced tomatoes
- Black beans
- Tortilla strips (optional)
- Lime
- Spices
- Salt and pepper

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Add chicken, onion, garlic, broth, tomatoes, and spices to the slow cooker.
2. Cook on low until chicken is tender.
3. Shred chicken in the pot, then stir in beans.
4. Simmer a bit longer to blend flavors.
5. Finish with lime juice and serve. Add tortilla strips if desired and log your portion in Eati.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 259 kcal per serving (estimated)
Fat: 10.1g
Protein: 27g

Tip: choose toppings wisely to keep calories and fat predictable.

sections.heading: Why Chicken Tortilla Soup Is Ideal for Weight Loss
sections.content:
Soup is one of the most underrated fat-loss foods. Research shows that soup consumption before or as a meal reduces overall daily calorie intake by 20%.

Why soup works for weight loss:
• High water content: Fills you up without adding calories.
• Slower eating: Spoon pace reduces meal speed by 30%+.
• Thermal effect: Hot soup slightly reduces appetite via temperature.
• Volume per calorie: 1 big bowl (1.5 cups) = 250–300 calories of highly filling food.

This recipe's macro strengths:
• 259 cal, 27g protein per serving
• 10g protein per 100 calories (excellent ratio)
• 10g fat (moderate, mostly from chicken thighs if used)
• 5–7g fiber from beans
• High in antioxidants (tomatoes, onion, garlic, peppers)

Comparison to canned/restaurant soups:
• Campbell's Chunky Chicken Tortilla Soup: 180 cal, 11g protein per cup.
• Chili's Chicken Tortilla Soup: 310 cal, 19g protein per bowl.
• Panera Chicken Tortilla Soup: 240 cal, 14g protein per cup.
• This recipe: 259 cal, 27g protein per bowl.

You get 50–100% more protein than restaurant versions. Use a protein calculator to set your daily target.

Bonus benefit: Soup freezes better than most foods, making it perfect for batch cooking and weeks of ready meals.

sections.heading: Building the Ultimate Tortilla Soup Base
sections.content:
The base broth is what separates mediocre from amazing tortilla soup.

Building layers of flavor:

Aromatic base (sautéed first):
• 1 large onion, diced
• 4 cloves garlic, minced
• 1 jalapeño, minced (optional)
• 1 red bell pepper, diced

Sauté in 1 tsp olive oil until soft and fragrant (5 min). This step is worth the extra effort — flavor compounds develop that you can't get from raw ingredients in the slow cooker.

Spice blend:
• 2 tsp cumin
• 1 tbsp chili powder
• 1 tsp smoked paprika
• 1 tsp dried oregano (preferably Mexican oregano)
• 1/2 tsp black pepper
• 1/4 tsp cayenne (adjust for heat)

Bloom spices in the aromatic mix for 30 seconds before adding liquid.

Liquid and flavor:
• 4 cups chicken broth (low-sodium)
• 1 can (14 oz) fire-roasted diced tomatoes
• 1 can (4 oz) diced green chiles
• 1 tsp lime zest (added later)
• Juice of 2 limes (added at end)

The secret ingredient: 1 tsp cocoa powder or a 1/2 oz square of unsweetened dark chocolate. Sounds weird but adds depth that makes people ask 'what's that amazing flavor?' — used in authentic mole sauces.

Optional umami boosters:
• 1 tsp fish sauce (undetectable, big flavor)
• 1 tbsp tomato paste
• Parmesan rind in broth (remove before serving)

sections.heading: Toppings: Where Calories Can Add Up Fast
sections.content:
Tortilla soup toppings range from near-zero to 300+ calories. Choose wisely.

Low-calorie toppings (under 30 cal):
• 2 tbsp chopped cilantro (1 cal)
• 1 tbsp diced red onion (4 cal)
• Fresh lime juice (2 cal)
• 1 tbsp pickled jalapeño (5 cal)
• 2 tbsp pico de gallo (15 cal)
• 2 tbsp crumbled cotija (40 cal, 3g protein)

Moderate toppings (30–80 cal):
• 2 tbsp Greek yogurt in place of sour cream (20 cal, 3g protein)
• 2 tbsp shredded Monterey Jack (55 cal)
• 1/8 sliced avocado (40 cal)
• 1 oz tortilla strips — baked (90 cal)

Higher-calorie toppings (80+ cal):
• 2 tbsp sour cream (60 cal)
• 1/4 avocado sliced (80 cal)
• 1 oz tortilla chips — fried (140 cal)
• 1/4 cup shredded cheese (110 cal)

The tortilla strip question:
Traditional tortilla soup has fried tortilla strips on top. At 140 cal per oz, they can nearly double the meal's calories.

Lower-calorie alternatives:
• Baked tortilla strips: 90 cal per oz. Cut 2 corn tortillas into strips, bake at 400°F for 8 min.
• Air-fried: 5 min at 400°F, even fewer calories.
• Half-portion: 0.5 oz of chips/strips = 70 cal.
• Skip and add pepitas: 1 tbsp roasted pepitas = 40 cal, extra crunch.

Recommended 'fat-loss' bowl:
259 cal soup + cilantro + lime + 2 tbsp Greek yogurt + 2 tbsp cotija + 0.5 oz baked tortilla strips = 390 cal, 32g protein. Excellent lunch or dinner.

sections.heading: Variations and International Inspirations
sections.content:
The tortilla soup base is versatile. Here are 6 variations:

1. Creamy chicken tortilla soup:
• Add 1 can (13.5 oz) light coconut milk at the end.
• Or stir in 1/2 cup Greek yogurt after slow cooker.
• Adds ~100 cal per serving but creates velvety texture.

2. White bean chicken soup:
• Swap black beans for white cannellini beans.
• Swap tomatoes for green salsa verde.
• Add 1/2 cup frozen corn.
• Milder flavor, still hits macros.

3. Pozole verde:
• Use hominy instead of black beans.
• Use salsa verde as main flavor.
• Top with radishes, shredded lettuce, lime.
• Classic Mexican recipe adaptation.

4. Southwestern chicken soup:
• Add 1 cup frozen corn.
• Add 1/2 cup roasted red peppers.
• Top with pepper jack cheese and jalapeños.

5. Creamy buffalo chicken chili:
• Sub buffalo sauce for salsa.
• Add 2 oz reduced-fat cream cheese at end.
• Top with blue cheese crumbles.

6. Taco soup (kid-friendly version):
• Reduce spice, sub ranch seasoning for chili.
• Add corn and extra beans.
• Top with mild cheddar and crushed tortilla chips.

Beanless version:
Skip beans, add 1 cup shredded cabbage at end. Drops recipe by 60 cal per serving, adds crunch. Great for keto or low-carb.

Vegetarian version:
Swap chicken for 2 cans of drained chickpeas and 1 can white beans. Use vegetable broth. ~210 cal, 14g protein per serving.

For related recipes, see [high protein chicken taco soup](/blog/high-protein-chicken-taco-soup-recipe), [slow cooker chili beef and pinto beans](/blog/slow-cooker-chili-beef-and-pinto-beans-recipe), and [fajita chicken casserole](/blog/fajita-chicken-casserole-recipe).

sections.heading: Meal Prep, Freezing, and Serving Tips
sections.content:
Tortilla soup is meal-prep gold — flavors improve for 2–3 days and it freezes beautifully.

Batch cooking:
• Double the recipe in 8-quart slow cooker.
• Same cooking time (low 6–8 hrs).
• Makes 10–12 servings.

Storage:
• Fridge: 5 days in airtight containers (flavor actually improves through day 3).
• Freezer: 4 months in portioned containers.

Freezer strategy:
• Freeze in wide-mouth jars (leave 1 inch headspace).
• Or use silicone ice cube trays for 1-cup portions.
• Freeze soup without toppings — add fresh when serving.

Thawing and reheating:
• Overnight in fridge (best).
• Defrost mode on microwave: 5 min per 2 cups, stir, heat another 3 min.
• Stovetop from frozen: medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, 15 min.

Serving suggestions:

Lunch portion:
• 1.5 cups soup (260 cal, 27g protein)
• Side salad with lime vinaigrette (80 cal)
• Total: 340 cal, 28g protein

Dinner portion:
• 2 cups soup (345 cal, 36g protein)
• Cornbread square, small (150 cal)
• Topped with cheese, yogurt, cilantro (80 cal)
• Total: 575 cal, 43g protein

Late-night snack:
• 1 cup soup warmed (175 cal, 18g protein)
• Small handful baked chips (40 cal)
• Satisfying, protein-rich evening option

Meal prep tip: Store soup and toppings separately. Pack toppings in small condiment containers so everything stays fresh. For more tracking guidance, see [how to track calories correctly](/blog/how-to-track-calories-correctly) and [how to eat out and still lose weight](/blog/how-to-eat-out-and-still-lose-weight).

conclusion: A great meal prep soup that helps you hit protein goals without long cooking time. Track it in Eati.
metaTitle: Slow Cooker Chicken Tortilla Soup: 27g Protein in 259 Calories (Mexican Comfort)
metaDescription: Easy slow cooker chicken tortilla soup with 27g protein per serving in just 259 calories. Includes flavor-building tips, low-calorie toppings, 6 variations, and meal prep freezer guide.
targetKeyword: slow cooker chicken tortilla soup high protein

faqs.question: How many calories in slow cooker chicken tortilla soup?
faqs.answer: Approximately 259 calories per serving (1.5 cups) with 27g of protein and 10g of fat. Adding typical toppings (Greek yogurt, cotija, baked tortilla strips) brings it to around 390 calories with 32g of protein.

faqs.question: Does chicken tortilla soup freeze well?
faqs.answer: Yes, exceptionally well. Freeze in portioned containers with 1 inch headspace for up to 4 months. Freeze soup without toppings; add fresh garnishes when serving. Flavor often improves after thawing as spices continue to meld.

faqs.question: Can I make this without a slow cooker?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Stovetop: Simmer covered for 45 minutes. Instant Pot: pressure cook 15 minutes with natural release. Dutch oven in 325°F oven: 1 hour covered. All methods work — the slow cooker is just most hands-off.

faqs.question: How do I make tortilla soup low-carb?
faqs.answer: Omit the black beans (saves ~60 cal and 20g carbs per serving) and skip the tortilla strips. Add extra shredded cabbage for volume. Top with avocado, cheese, sour cream, and lime. This version has ~6g net carbs per serving.

faqs.question: Are tortilla strips necessary?
faqs.answer: No, they're traditional but not essential. If you want crunch without the calories, use baked tortilla strips (90 cal per oz vs 140 for fried), roasted pepitas (40 cal per tbsp), or crumbled baked cassava chips. Or skip crunch entirely and add extra veggies.
